title: ageratina subinclusa
intro: Ageratina subinclusa, also known as Small-flowered Mountain Mint, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ageratina subinclusa include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Ageratina subinclusa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
